title: Writing: Modals Matrix
learningGoal: Construct sentences using Can, Should, and Have To, shifting between normal subjects and dative subjects as required.
instructions: Translate these sentences. If the sentence uses 'can', use 'Main'. If the sentence uses 'should' or 'have to', use 'Mujhe/Tumhein'. Pay attention to gender!
writingPrompt:
Translate the following sentences into Hindi (Assume MALE speaker):
1. I can do work. (Work = Kaam [Masc], Do = Kar)
2. I should do work. 
3. You (casual) will have to go. (Go = Jaana)
4. I have to go now. (Now = Ab)
5. I cannot drink tea. (Tea = Chai [Fem])
promptGoal: Accurately deploy sakna, chahiye, padega, and immediate compulsion.

modelExampleOutputs.targetText: मैं काम कर सकता हूँ। मुझे काम करना चाहिए। तुम्हें जाना पड़ेगा। मुझे अब जाना है। मैं चाय नहीं पी सकता।
modelExampleOutputs.transliteration: Main kaam kar sakta hoon. Mujhe kaam karna chahiye. Tumhein jaana padega. Mujhe ab jaana hai. Main chai nahi pee sakta.
modelExampleOutputs.baseTranslation: I can do work. I should do work. You will have to go. I have to go now. I cannot drink tea.
modelExampleOutputs.annotation: Perfect shifting. 'Main' is used for 'sakta'. 'Mujhe' and 'Tumhein' are used for 'chahiye', 'padega', and 'hai'.

evaluationCriteria.description: Must use 'Main' for sentences 1 and 5. Must use 'Mujhe' for 2 and 4. Must use 'Tumhein' for 3.
Must use 'sakta' for can, 'chahiye' for should, 'padega' for will have to, and 'hai' for immediate compulsion.
Must use root verb 'kar' before 'sakta', but full verb 'karna/jaana' before 'chahiye/padega/hai'.

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: मैं काम कर सकता हूँ। मुझे काम करना चाहिए। तुम्हें जाना पड़ेगा। मुझे अब जाना है। मैं चाय नहीं पी सकता।
acceptableAlternates: Main kaam kar sakta hoon. Mujhe kaam karna chahiye. Tumhein jaana padega. Mujhe ab jaana hai. Main chai nahi pee sakta.
main kaam kar sakta hoon. mujhe kaam karna chahiye. tumhein jaana padega. mujhe ab jaana hai. main chai nahi pee sakta.
adminCorrectAnswerSet.grammarBaseline: Accepts transliteration. Strict check on Main vs Mujhe.

criteria.name: Dative Failure
criteria.description: If user writes 'Main kaam karna chahiye' or 'Main jaana hai', score drops massively.

feedbackRules.onPass: Flawless execution! You shifted between the Absolutive (Main) and Dative (Mujhe) cases perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review Section 6. 'Sakna' (can) is the only one that uses 'Main'. All the others (Should, Have to) MUST use 'Mujhe'.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try. Look at Sentence 4. To say 'I have to go', you don't use 'chahiye', you just say 'Mujhe jaana hai'.

hintText: Sentence 5 uses 'Sakna', so it reverts back to the normal subject 'Main'.
commonMistakes: Writing 'Main jaana padega' (You must use Mujhe).
